rules.rule: Follow OOP and SOLID. Value objects are immutable: declare them with @dataclass(frozen=True) and validate invariants on construction. Prefer composition over inheritance, and depend on abstractions (ABC or Protocol) injected through the constructor rather than on concrete classes.
Domain value objects such as FuzzyTriangleNumber and ExpertOpinion must be immutable and fully validated when constructed. Flag added setters, in-place mutation, or fields left unvalidated.

Every median calculation strategy must fully implement the strategy contract for both the odd and the even expert-count case. Flag a strategy subclass that leaves a branch unimplemented or silently returns a partial result.
Subclasses of the base aggregation calculator must honor the template-method skeleton: call the defined steps in order and do not skip a step that affects the computed result.

Keep endpoints thin. Business logic belongs in service classes injected via Annotated[Service, Depends(get_service)] -- never instantiate a service directly inside a route. Accept and return Pydantic schemas, not raw SQLModel table models. Raise custom domain exceptions rather than a bare HTTPException.

Service-layer errors must raise domain-specific exceptions (for example UserExistsError, ProjectNotFoundError), never a bare Exception or ValueError. Document every raised exception in the docstring with :raises:.
Services collaborate through constructor injection (composition), not by inheriting from one another. The only inheritance allowed is from BaseService for session access.

Storage backends are interchangeable (Railway bucket, S3, local). Any implementation must satisfy the full storage Protocol with matching method signatures; flag a missing or diverging method that would only fail at runtime.

Translations must stay parallel: every key added under locales/en must have a matching key with identical structure under locales/cs, and vice versa. Flag a key added to one language but missing in the other.
User-facing text, including aria-label and placeholder values, must come from i18n via t() or tCommon. Flag hardcoded display-string literals in components and pages.
Screen-reader-only content must use the Tailwind sr-only class, not a hand-rolled visually-hidden style. Radix UI already provides the ARIA base, so do not reinvent accessibility primitives.
When a component's primary namespace is not common, general UI text (buttons, labels, validation messages) should be read through tCommon, reserving t for namespace-specific keys.
Use absolute imports only (for example: from api.services.case_service import CaseService). Flag relative imports such as from .services import ... -- they are not caught by the linter.

Public classes, methods, and functions need a reStructuredText docstring with :param:, :return:, and :raises: as applicable. The docstring states what the object does and how to use it, not its internal implementation.
Keep code, comments, and docstrings ASCII-only. Use -> instead of an arrow glyph, -- instead of an em dash, and straight quotes instead of typographic quotes.

Unit tests must isolate the component under test by mocking external dependencies -- database session, other services, and storage. A unit test that opens a real Session() or hits the database is an integration test and belongs under tests/integration.
